description: Jonathan Duff started his role as Regional Director for the East of England in September 2022. He was previously the Regional Schools Commissioner for East of England and North-East London. Prior to this, Jonathan was Director of Services and Operations for the Department for Education Regional Delivery Directorate. Before that, he was Director of the Regional Schools Commissioner’s Office for South-East England and the South of London. Earlier in his career, Jonathan has also worked on: delivery of the academies programme reforms to teachers’ pay and conditions legislation for young people aged 16 to 25 with special educational needs qualifications delivery and curriculum policy

details.body:
Jonathan Duff started his role as Regional Director for the East of England in September 2022.

He was previously the Regional Schools Commissioner for East of England and North-East London.

Prior to this, Jonathan was Director of Services and Operations for the Department for Education Regional Delivery Directorate. Before that, he was Director of the Regional Schools Commissioner’s Office for South-East England and the South of London.

Earlier in his career, Jonathan has also worked on:

  • delivery of the academies programme
  • reforms to teachers’ pay and conditions
  • legislation for young people aged 16 to 25 with special educational needs
  • qualifications delivery and curriculum policy

Jonathan Duff started his role as Regional Director for the East of England in September 2022.

He was previously the Regional Schools Commissioner for East of England and North-East London.

Prior to this, Jonathan was Director of Services and Operations for the Department for Education Regional Delivery Directorate. Before that, he was Director of the Regional Schools Commissioner’s Office for South-East England and the South of London.

Earlier in his career, Jonathan has also worked on:

  • delivery of the academies programme
  • reforms to teachers’ pay and conditions
  • legislation for young people aged 16 to 25 with special educational needs
  • qualifications delivery and curriculum policy

The Interim Regional Director for the West Midlands is responsible for working locally across children’s social care, SEND, schools and area-based programmes to improve outcomes for children, families and learners. They are responsible for making decisions in the following local authorities:

  • Birmingham
  • Coventry
  • Dudley
  • Herefordshire, County of
  • Sandwell
  • Shropshire
  • Solihull
  • Staffordshire
  • Stoke-on-Trent
  • Telford and Wrekin
  • Walsall
  • Warwickshire
  • Wolverhampton
  • Worcestershire

More information about the responsibilities of regional directors is available.

Read about the West Midlands advisory board.

If you have any questions, email the regional director’s office rg.wm@education.gov.uk.

role.title: Interim Regional Director for West Midlands

role_appointments.title: Jonathan Duff - Interim Regional Director for West Midlands

details.body:
Jonathan Duff started his role as Regional Director for the East of England in September 2022.

He was previously the Regional Schools Commissioner for East of England and North-East London.

Prior to this, Jonathan was Director of Services and Operations for the Department for Education Regional Delivery Directorate. Before that, he was Director of the Regional Schools Commissioner’s Office for South-East England and the South of London.

Earlier in his career, Jonathan has also worked on:

  • delivery of the academies programme
  • reforms to teachers’ pay and conditions
  • legislation for young people aged 16 to 25 with special educational needs
  • qualifications delivery and curriculum policy

The Regional Schools Commissioner (RSC) for the East of England and North-East London is responsible for making decisions about the academies and free schools in the following local authorities:

  • Barking and Dagenham
  • Cambridgeshire
  • Essex
  • Hackney
  • Haringey
  • Havering
  • Newham
  • Norfolk
  • Peterborough
  • Redbridge
  • Southend-on-Sea
  • Suffolk
  • Thurrock
  • Tower Hamlets
  • Waltham Forest

More information about the responsibilities of RSCs is available.

Read about the East of England and north-east London advisory board.

If you have any questions email the commissioner’s office at rsc.eastnelondon@education.gov.uk

Twitter: @RSC_EENEL
